# Kotak — Usage Guide

*Mode: direct · 🔌 offline (no LLM)*

Turns a Kotak Mahindra Bank savings-account transaction-statement PDF into a flat CSV — one row per transaction. Runs fully offline (no LLM, no internet) and reads the statement's ruled/bordered table structure directly, so a table that overflows from one page to the next (without repeating the column header) is still captured correctly, and the trailing abbreviation-legend page is excluded rather than mistaken for transaction rows.

## When to use it

Use this when you have a Kotak savings-account statement as a PDF and want it as a tidy CSV you can open in Excel, reconcile, or feed into the GnuCash Import pipeline (choose "Kotak" there to do extraction + account mapping in one step instead). This skill is extraction only — it does not categorise or assign accounts.

## Inputs

- **Kotak statement PDF** (required) — accepts: PDF (.pdf) — one statement per run.
  - Your Kotak statement PDF.
  - ⚠️ Use the bank's original text PDF, not a photo/scan. Password-protected PDFs must be unlocked first, or the run will report the file could not be opened.

## How to run

1. Click the input and pick a Kotak statement PDF.
2. Click Run — it processes offline in a few seconds.
3. Download the CSV, or open it from the outputs folder (see below).

## Output

Output is written to the **`Data/outputs/`** folder, named:

```
Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-Kotak.csv
```

Files produced:

- **`YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-Kotak.csv`** — One row per transaction with columns: #, Date, Description, Chq/Ref. No., Withdrawal, Deposit, Balance. Rows are in statement order; the "Opening Balance" front-matter row and the trailing abbreviation-legend page are both excluded from the CSV.

## Tips

The running balance carries straight from the statement — if the final Balance matches your passbook, the extraction tied out. Sweep-in/sweep-out transfers to a linked Fixed Deposit are kept as real transactions, not filtered out. Columns are kept verbatim from the statement so amounts are not rounded or reformatted.

## Troubleshooting

| If… | Then… |
|------|-------|
| The output CSV is empty or has very few rows. | The PDF is probably a scanned image, not a text PDF. Re-download the original statement from Kotak net-banking, or run it through an OCR step first. This skill does not OCR. |
| Run fails saying the PDF could not be opened. | The PDF is password-protected. Unlock it (open in a viewer, print/save as a new unprotected PDF) and try again. |
| Closing balance in the CSV does not match my passbook. | Check the statement's last page — the abbreviation legend on that page is intentionally excluded, but if any real transaction rows sit above it, make sure the whole document (all pages) was included when you selected the PDF. |
